spec_match.rationale: Uses `scroll-target-group: highlight` and toggles it, but `highlight` is not a value in the spec — the spec lists `none` and `auto`. The page invented a value.

interactive.rationale: 7 interactive markers — jump-between buttons, toggle controls, real scroll behaviour.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises toggle group highlighting; the page delivers (modulo the invented value name).

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't demonstrate nesting (group inside group), or what happens with fragment navigation while group is active.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus ID `4549035007868928` — different feature. Correct id is `5189126177161216`. Breaks invariant 3.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus link ID
openQuestions.detail: see-also links chromestatus.com/feature/4549035007868928 — wrong feature. Correct ID: 5189126177161216. The slug-cleanup script will delete this folder otherwise.

openQuestions.title: Verify scroll-target-group values
openQuestions.detail: The page toggles a `highlight` value. The chromestatus summary lists `none` and `auto`. Confirm against the latest CSS Overflow spec which values actually exist — if the page is using a stale syntax it teaches wrong behaviour.

openQuestions.title: Show :target-current vs :target
openQuestions.detail: These pseudos are the consumers of scroll-target-group. Demonstrate both side-by-side here as the canonical pattern.

summary: Interactive enough but technically suspect — the page uses an invented value ('highlight') and links the wrong chromestatus feature. Verify against spec before merging.
